chapters.title: Digital Fingerprints
chapters.storyPurpose: Morrison shows Sam the first compromised system and explains how containers work like isolated crime scenes
chapters.learningPurpose: Understanding what containers are and why they provide isolation
chapters.docQuery: what is a container docker basics isolation
chapters.narrativeSetup: Morrison leads you to a terminal displaying the city's traffic management system, now showing error messages instead of green status lights. He points to the logs: 'This is where it started. Three days ago, this container went dark.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: You run your first container successfully, watching it spin up in isolation. Morrison nods approvingly: 'Now you're thinking like the Architect. Every container is a separate world. That's how they're staying hidden.'

chapters.title: The Blueprint
chapters.storyPurpose: Sam discovers the saboteur is using custom container images and learns to examine them like evidence
chapters.learningPurpose: Creating and understanding Docker images and Dockerfiles
chapters.narrativeSetup: In the Archive, you find backup drives from the compromised systems. Morrison hands you a forensic imaging tool: 'The Architect didn't just break these systems—they rebuilt them. We need to see their blueprint.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: Your custom image builds successfully, and you can see exactly how the Architect modified the original city systems. Morrison studies your analysis: 'They're not just vandalizing—they're improving the code. But why?'

chapters.title: The Network
chapters.storyPurpose: The team discovers how the saboteur is moving between systems through container networks
chapters.docQuery: docker network port mapping container communication
chapters.narrativeSetup: Maria Santos calls from Emergency Services: 'The failures aren't random—they're connected. Something is jumping between our isolated systems.' Morrison turns to you: 'Time to map the digital crime scene.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: You trace the network connections between compromised containers, revealing a hidden pathway through the city's infrastructure. Morrison sees the pattern: 'They're not attacking systems—they're building something.'

chapters.title: Persistent Evidence
chapters.storyPurpose: Sam realizes the Architect is leaving permanent traces in container volumes and must learn to preserve evidence
chapters.docQuery: docker volumes persistent data bind mounts
chapters.narrativeSetup: The Emergency Services grid flickers and dies. In the chaos, you notice something Morrison missed: the Architect's containers are writing data somewhere permanent. 'They're not just passing through,' you tell Morrison. 'They're leaving something behind.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: You mount the persistent volumes and find the Architect's true message—detailed security reports on every system they've 'attacked.' Morrison's expression hardens: 'They're not trying to break the city. They're trying to save it.'

chapters.title: The Orchestration
chapters.storyPurpose: The final system failure reveals the Architect's master plan—a coordinated demonstration using Docker Compose
chapters.narrativeSetup: Every screen in the command center goes red simultaneously. This isn't another isolated failure—it's a coordinated shutdown of the entire emergency grid. Morrison grabs his coat: 'They're making their final move. We need to get to the rooftop facility now.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: You deploy your own compose configuration to counter the Architect's orchestrated demonstration, bringing the emergency systems back online one by one. In the process, you realize the Architect has been teaching you—every 'attack' was a lesson in container security.

chapters.title: The Architect's Gambit
chapters.storyPurpose: Sam confronts Jake Williams and uses everything learned to secure the city's container infrastructure
chapters.learningPurpose: Applying all Docker concepts together in a real-world scenario
chapters.docQuery: docker security best practices production deployment
chapters.narrativeSetup: On the rooftop, you find Jake 'Docker' Williams waiting by the servers, rain streaming down his face. 'I wondered when you'd figure it out,' he says. 'The city's infrastructure is a disaster waiting to happen. I had to show them.'
chapters.narrativePayoff: Using containers, images, networks, volumes, and orchestration, you implement Jake's security improvements properly—without the dramatic failures. Morrison watches the city's systems stabilize: 'You just saved Metro City, Sam. And maybe taught an old detective some new tricks.'

narrativeThread: Sam must master Docker container technology to trace a saboteur who's systematically compromising Metro City's infrastructure, only to discover that the 'Architect' is actually exposing critical security flaws to force necessary improvements. Each technical skill learned becomes a piece of evidence in understanding the saboteur's true motivation and methods.
resolutionPlan: The final confrontation requires Sam to use containers for isolation, custom images for deployment, networks for communication, volumes for persistence, and compose for orchestration—all the skills from previous chapters—to implement the Architect's security improvements correctly and restore the city's systems while addressing the underlying vulnerabilities that motivated the attacks.
